title: Bruins ‘comfortable’ taking a leap of faith on two goalie prospects in 2026 NHL Draft
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins selected goalies Yuri Ivanov and Roberto Henriquez in the 2026 NHL Draft.
Ivanov, a Russian goalie, was picked 56th overall; Henriquez will play for Boston College.
Bruins' focus on goalies aims to strengthen future roster depth, despite defensemen needs.

title: Extreme heat is gripping New England. An ER doctor shares how to stay safe.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: New England is experiencing dangerous heat, leading to increased emergency room visits for heat-related illnesses.
Dr. Andrew Eyre from Brigham and Women’s Hospital advises cooling strategies for heat stroke, like using ice packs and fans.
Children, elderly, and people with chronic health issues are more vulnerable to heat; hydration and indoor cooling are crucial.

title: Market Basket named a top employer in latest U.S. News & World Report list
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Market Basket has been named one of the best consumer products companies to work for by U.S. News & World Report.
The Tewksbury-based grocery chain, employing 32,000 people, is recognized for its employee benefits and positive work environment.
Market Basket's former CEO Arthur T. Demoulas was ousted after a contentious corporate power struggle with his sisters.

title: Revolution still chasing permanent move for Matt Turner, possible Designated Player acquisition
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Chris Tierney appointed as New England Revolution's general manager, replacing Curt Onalfo who focuses on player development.
New England Revolution seeks to retain goalkeeper Matt Turner, on loan from Lyon, beyond initial one-year agreement.
The team considers signing a Designated Player to strengthen the roster during the MLS summer transfer window.

title: One charged with OUI after woman allegedly fell from the roof of a moving car in South Boston
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Brandon Harper, 38, charged with OUI after woman fell from car roof in South Boston.
The woman was seriously injured after falling while allegedly smoking on the moving car's roof.
Harper's breath test showed a BAC over three times the legal limit; court set bail at $10,000.

title: Boston magazine names Cambridge’s Fallow Kin the Best New Restaurant of 2026
author: Emily Turner
bullets: Boston magazine awarded Cambridge's Fallow Kin as the Best New Restaurant in its 2026 Best of Boston awards.
Fallow Kin emphasizes sustainably sourced, vegetable-driven dishes and opened last fall at 853 Main St. in Cambridge.
The restaurant collaboration includes Danielle Ayer, Conor Dennehy, and chef Marcos Sanchez, known for creative uses of vegetables.

title: Suspect flees after crowd throws drinks at lone BPD officer, prompting renewed staffing worries
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: A suspect escaped a Boston police officer's arrest attempt after a crowd threw drinks and items at the officer.
Boston Police Patrolmen’s Association demanded higher staffing, citing the attack as life-threatening due to understaffing issues.
Boston faces staffing challenges amid increased police resignations and retirements; department operates below required patrol numbers.

title: What you need to know about Boston Harborfest
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Boston Harborfest, from July 2-4, will host free historical reenactments, live music, and fireworks across multiple city locations.
MBTA fares will be free after 8:30 p.m. for the July 4 fireworks show, with modified schedules on public transport.
The Boston Pops Fireworks Spectacular on July 4 will feature performances by Lainey Wilson, Chance The Rapper, and more.

title: WCVB reshuffles weather lineup with Mike Wankum’s retirement
author: John Waller
bullets: Meteorologist Mike Wankum retires, prompting WCVB to implement weather lineup changes this week.
Cindy Fitzgibbon moves to weeknight meteorologist role, leaving her previous weekday morning position.
Kelly Ann Cicalese transitions from weekend meteorologist to Cindy Fitzgibbon's former weekday morning role.

title: Boston-area restaurant chain takes Storrowing as opportunity to apologize, and plug their pizza
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: A Sal's Pizza truck got 'Storrowed,' stuck under an overpass on Storrow Drive, disrupting traffic.
Sal's Pizza issued a humorous apology on Facebook and used the incident to promote their jumbo pizza slices.
Founded in 1990, Sal's Pizza now has about 130 locations across New England and one in Florida.
